author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke xvii. 3, ‘If thy brother sin, rebuke him,’ etc., touching the remission of sins.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xvii. 3, ‘If thy brother sin, rebuke him,’ etc., touching the remission of sins.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Delivered at the Table of St. Cyprian, in the presence of Count Boniface.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Holy Gospel which we heard just now as it was being read, has admonished touching the remission of sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And on this subject must ye be admonished now by my discourse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are ministers of the word, not our own word, but the word of our God and Lord, whom no one serves without glory, whom no one despises without punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then the Lord our God, who abiding with the Father made us, and having been made for us, re-made us, He the Lord our God Jesus Christ Himself says to us what we have heard just now in the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If,” He saith, “thy brother shall sin against thee, rebuke him, and if he shall repent, forgive him; and if he shall sin against time seven times in a day, and shall come and say, I repent, forgive him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would not have “seven times in a day” otherwise understood than “as often as may be,” lest haply he sin eight times, and thou be unwilling to forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is “seven times”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Always, as often as he shall sin and repent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this, “Seven times in a day will I praise thee,” is the same as in another Psalm, “His praise shall always be in my mouth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And there is the strongest reason why seven times should be put for that which is always: for the whole course of time revolves in a circle of seven coming and returning days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever then thou art that hast thy thoughts on Christ, and desirest to receive what He hath promised, be not slow to do that which He hath enjoined.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what hath He promised?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Eternal life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what hath He enjoined?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That pardon be given to thy brother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As if He had said to thee, “Do thou, O man, give pardon to a man, that I, who am God, may come unto thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that I may pass over, or rather pass by for a while, those more exalted divine promises in which our Creator engages to make us equal with His Angels, that we may with Him, and in Him, and by Him, live without end; not to speak of this just now, dost thou not wish to receive of thy God this very thing, which thou art commanded to give thy brother?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This very thing, I say, which thou art commanded to give thy brother, dost thou not wish to receive from thy Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me if thou wishest it not; and so give it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this, but that thou shouldest forgive him that asks thee, if thou require to be forgiven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou have nothing to be forgiven thee, I dare to say, be unwilling to forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though I ought not even to say this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though thou have nothing to be forgiven thee, forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art just on the point of saying to me, “But I am not God, I am a man, a sinner.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God be thanked that thou dost confess thou hast sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forgive then, that they may be forgiven thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet the Lord Himself our God exhorteth us to imitate Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the first place God Himself, Christ, exhorteth us, of whom the Apostle Peter said, “Christ hath suffered for us, leaving you an example that ye should follow His steps, who did no sin, neither was guile, found in His mouth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then verily had no sin, yet did He die for our sins, and shed His Blood for the remission of sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He took upon Him for our sakes what was not His due, that He might deliver us from what was due to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Death was not due to Him, nor life to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because we were sinners.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Death was not due to Him, nor life to us; He received what was not due to Him, He gave what was not due to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But since we are speaking of the remission of sins, lest ye should think it too high a thing to imitate Christ, hear the Apostle saying, “Forgiving one another, even as God in Christ hath forgiven you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Be ye therefore imitators of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the Apostle’s words, not mine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it indeed a proud thing to imitate God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle, “Be ye imitators of God as dearly beloved children.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art called a child: if thou refuse to imitate Him, why seekest thou His inheritance?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This would I say even if thou hadst no sin which thou mightest desire to be forgiven thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as it is, whosoever thou art, thou art a man; though thou be righteous, thou art a man; be thou layman, or monk, or clerk, or Bishop, or Apostle, thou art a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle’s voice, “If we shall say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He, that famous John and an Evangelist, he whom the Lord Christ loved beyond all the rest, who lay on His breast, he says, “If we shall say.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “If ye shall say that ye have no sin,” but “if we shall say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He joined himself in the guilt, that he might be joined in the pardon also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If we shall say.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider who it is that says, “If we shall say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if we shall confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all iniquity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How does He cleanse?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By forgiving, not as though He found nothing to punish, but as finding something to forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, Brethren, if we have sins, let us forgive them that ask us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not retain enmities in our heart against another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the retaining of enmities more than anything corrupts this heart of ours.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would then that thou shouldest forgive, seeing that I find thee asking forgiveness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art asked, forgive: thou art asked, and thou wilt ask thyself; thou art asked, forgive; thou wilt ask to be forgiven; for, lo, the time of prayer will come: I have thee fast in the words thou wilt have to speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt say, “Our Father, which art in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou wilt not be in the number of children, if thou shalt not say, “Our Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then thou wilt say, “Our Father, which art in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Follow on; “Hallowed be Thy Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say on, “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Follow still on, “Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what thou addest next, “Give us this day our daily bread.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where are thy riches?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So thou art a beggar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless in the mean while (it is the point I am speaking of), say what is next after, “Give us this day our daily bread.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say what follows this: “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now thou hast come to my words, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By what right? by what covenant? on what condition? on what express stipulation?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “As we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is but a small thing that thou dost not forgive; yea thou dost more, thou liest unto God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The condition is laid down, the law fixed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive as I forgive.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore He does not forgive, unless thou forgivest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive as I forgive.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wishest to be forgiven when thou askest, forgive him that asks of thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that is skilled in heaven’s laws has dictated these prayers: He does not deceive thee; ask according to the tenor of His heavenly voice: say, “Forgive us, as we also forgive,” and do what thou sayest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that lies in his prayers, loses the benefit he seeks: he that lies in his prayers, both loses his cause, and finds his punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if any one lies to the emperor, he is convicted of his lie at his coming: but when thou liest in prayer, thou by thy very prayer art convicted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God does not seek for witness as regards thee to convict thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who dictated the prayers to thee, is thine Advocate: if thou liest, He is a witness against thee: if thou dost not amend thyself, He will be thy Judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then both say it, and do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou say it not, thou wilt not obtain making thy requests contrary to the law; but if thou say it and do it not, thou wilt be further guilty of lying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is no means of evading that verse, save by fulfilling what we say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Can we blot this verse out of our prayer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would ye that clause, “Forgive us our debts,” should be there, and that we should blot out what follows, “As we also forgive our debtors”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou shalt not blot it out, lest thou be first blotted out thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then in this prayer thou sayest, “Give,” and thou sayest, “Forgive:” that thou mayest receive what thou hast not, and may be forgiven what thou hast done amiss.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then thou wishest to receive, give; thou wishest to be forgiven, forgive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a brief summary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Christ Himself in another place, “Forgive, and ye shall be forgiven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What will ye forgive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What others have sinned against you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall ye be forgiven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What ye have sinned yourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give, and there shall be given you what ye desire,” eternal life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Support the temporal life of the poor man, sustain the poor man’s present life, and for this so small and earthly seed ye shall receive for harvest life eternal.
